title: One-Shot Bodies, Streaming, Uploads, Decompression, and Resource Limits · Browser and N… · Type-System Trace: Message Delimitation Trailers
summary: A media uploader streams files and reads large responses through Web Streams without accidental cloning, locked bodies, or unbounded decoding. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts message delimitation trailers increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch bounded streaming body pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reason about known lengths, HTTP/1.1 chunked delimitation, end of stream, and trailers without exposing framing details as semantics. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Stream large uploads with known ownership, backpressure, progress, cancellation, and retryability. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Predict Message Delimitation Trailers · Theory Message Delimitation Trailers
steps.instruction:
